About Us

A Foundation Rooted in
Rural India's Heart

Registered as a Section 8 Company under the Companies Act 2013 — India's highest standard for non-profit governance. Our purpose is anchored in the villages of Western Uttar Pradesh, where need is greatest and resources are fewest.

We work at the intersection of human dignity and ecological responsibility — running village schools, nutrition programmes, sanitation, rural women's upliftment, skill development centres, tree plantation campaigns, and wildlife conservation.

🏛️ Legal Identity

Section 8 Company under Companies Act 2013 — India's gold standard for non-profit accountability and transparency.

🗺️ Our Reach

Rooted in villages of Western Uttar Pradesh, scaling an integrated rural development model nationally.

🏛️
Registered Non-Profit · Section 8 CompanyAccountable, transparent, and community-driven in all we do
Our Purpose

Mission & Vision

Guided by science, driven by compassion — every action rooted in purpose for people and planet.

🎯

Our Mission

To foster holistic upliftment of rural communities — particularly women and children — through quality education, nutrition, sanitation, skill development, and healthcare; while promoting environmental conservation, tree plantation, and wildlife protection. A flourishing human community and a healthy natural environment are inseparable goals, and we work every day to build both.

🌅

Our Vision

A self-reliant rural India where every girl child has quality education, every rural woman is economically empowered and socially dignified, every village has clean water and proper sanitation, and every community lives in harmony with its natural environment — villages that are green, educated, healthy, and prosperous.

Leadership

About the Founder

A scientist who chose the village over the laboratory, and service over recognition.

AT
Arjun Tyagi
Founder · Healthy Earth Foundation
🎓
PhD Scholar — Environmental Engineering
Atmospheric Science & Air Quality Research
🏅
M.Tech — Environmental Engineering
Postgraduate in Environmental Systems
🔬
Researcher · CSIR-NEERI, Nagpur
National Environmental Engineering Research Institute
🇮🇳
Serving the Nation · 24×7
Without personal benefit or recognition

His Story

Arjun Tyagi is a PhD scholar and M.Tech in Environmental Engineering, currently serving as a researcher at CSIR-NEERI (National Environmental Engineering Research Institute), Nagpur — one of India's most prestigious environmental science institutions under the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research.

A scientist by training and a servant by choice, Arjun founded Healthy Earth Foundation with one conviction: that real, lasting change in India begins in the village. With deep expertise in air quality modelling, atmospheric dispersion, particulate matter analysis, and environmental impact assessment, he brings rigorous scientific thinking to every community programme the Foundation runs.

He works tirelessly — 24 hours a day, 7 days a week — without personal gain, driven only by his love for the land, its people, and their future. For Arjun, science and service are not separate paths. They converge in the village school, the sapling drive, and the women's skill centre.

🌬️ Air Quality Research 🌾 Rural Development 📚 Girl Child Education 🌳 Tree Plantation 🦚 Wildlife Conservation 💧 Sanitation & Water

"I did not choose between science and service. The village taught me they are one and the same. Every air quality reading I take, every child who learns to read under our school's roof — both are acts of healing the Earth."

— Arjun Tyagi · Founder, Healthy Earth Foundation
Focus Areas

Nine Pillars of Our Work

From the classroom to the forest — nine interconnected domains of human and planetary wellbeing.

👧

Girl Child Education

Village schools in Western UP with focus on enrolment, retention, and quality outcomes for girls.

👩‍🌾

Rural Women's Upliftment

Self-help groups, legal literacy, and social empowerment for dignity and rights of rural women.

🛠️

Skill Development

Vocational training — stitching, handicrafts, digital literacy, and entrepreneurship.

🤱

Child Nutrition

Mid-day meal support, anaemia awareness, and mother-child nutrition camps in villages.

🚿

Sanitation & Hygiene

Girls' toilets in schools, menstrual health education, and clean water access drives.

🌳

Tree Plantation

Seasonal drives planting native species — greening village commons, schools, and roadsides.

🦚

Wildlife Conservation

Community awareness, anti-poaching education, and habitat protection.

🌿

Environment Awareness

Village campaigns on air, water, and soil conservation through schools and panchayats.

🏫

Rural School Development

Infrastructure, digital classrooms, libraries, and teacher support for village schools.

MOA Objectives

Our Constitutional Commitments

As enshrined in our Memorandum of Association — guiding every programme we run.

01

Education for All

Establish schools, libraries, and digital learning centres with emphasis on girl child education in rural areas.

02

Rural Women's Upliftment

Promote social, economic, and educational advancement of rural women through SHGs and skills training.

03

Skill & Livelihood

Vocational and entrepreneurial training to build dignified, sustainable livelihoods for rural youth and women.

04

Child Welfare & Nutrition

Eliminate child malnutrition, support mid-day meals, and conduct mother-child health campaigns.

05

Sanitation & Water

Safe water access, sanitation facilities, hygiene education, and menstrual health awareness.

06

Environmental Conservation

Tree plantation, biodiversity conservation, environmental awareness, and ecological restoration.

Why Choose Us

Science-Backed,
Community-Led

Our founder and teams live among the communities we serve — combining rigorous science with deep human commitment and grassroots trust.

🏘️

Grassroots Presence

Our teams live in Western UP villages — no outsider programmes, no top-down approaches.

🔬

Scientific Leadership

Founded by a PhD Environmental Engineer with expertise at CSIR-NEERI, Nagpur.

👩

Women-Led Action

Local women serve as coordinators — genuine community ownership and trust.

📊

Full Transparency

Section 8 compliance, annual disclosures, field-verified outcome reporting.

🌱

Integrated Approach

Women's empowerment, education, skill development, and ecology in one mission.

🤝

Panchayat Partnership

Deep collaboration with gram panchayats ensures long-term sustainability.

Methodology

Four Steps to Lasting Change

A science-informed cycle turning community insight into scalable, lasting impact on the ground.

01

Assess

Field surveys and community meetings to understand real needs and local strengths.

02

Design

Co-create solutions with villagers and women's groups — never top-down.

03

Implement

Execute with local hands — training village women and youth as agents of lasting change.

04

Monitor & Scale

Transparent evaluation and replication of proven models across more villages.

Technology We Deploy

📡 Remote Sensing & GIS
🤖 AI & Data Analytics
📲 Mobile Data Collection
🧪 Environmental Lab Analysis
🛰️ Satellite Monitoring
📊 Impact Dashboards
Our Commitments

Promises We Keep,
Every Single Day

  • ✓Full financial transparency — annual public accounts per Section 8 Company norms
  • ✓Every donation channelled directly to village programmes — zero administrative leakage
  • ✓Rural women and girl children at the centre of every programme we design and deliver
  • ✓Community-led design — villagers, panchayats, and women's groups shape every initiative
  • ✓One sapling planted for every child enrolled in our school development programmes
  • ✓Independent evaluation of all major programmes — verified impact, no greenwashing
  • ✓Full dignity, privacy, and data protection for all beneficiaries at all times
"Service to the Earth and service to its people are not two different things. They are one and the same act of love — chosen every single day."
— Healthy Earth Foundation
Our Partners

Together We Are Stronger

Seven categories of partners bring unique strengths to our shared mission of rural upliftment and environmental stewardship.

Gram Panchayats and state government departments open institutional doors, provide land for plantation drives, and integrate our programmes into Swachh Bharat, Poshan Abhiyan, and Skill India.

Be Part of Something
That Truly Matters

Whether you donate, volunteer, or partner with us — your involvement changes a child's life, empowers a rural woman, plants a tree, and heals the Earth.

We read every message and respond within 48 hours.

Section 8 Company · Non-Profit

A registered non-profit dedicated to rural women's upliftment, child welfare, skill development, and environmental conservation. Founded by Arjun Tyagi, PhD Scholar, CSIR-NEERI Nagpur.
